paragraphs.en: Are the Enhanced Games an existential threat to fairness or a cure for death?
paragraphs.zh: 增强者奥林匹克是对公平性的一种生存威胁，还是一剂解药？

paragraphs.en: The largest person I met at the Enhanced Games, a mini Olympics for steroid users that was held in Las Vegas this past May, was a six-foot-ten, three-hundred-and-twenty-pound Russian man named Lukas Lakutsin. He was having lunch near the pool at the Fontainebleau. He wore a tank top, and I was staring, perhaps inappropriately, at the veins in his forearms. I shook his hand. I felt like a newborn clasping onto his father. The pecs, the traps. I asked him what he’d ordered to eat. He replied, “Everything.”
paragraphs.zh: 在上月于拉斯维加斯举办的增强者奥林匹克——一个为类固醇使用者准备的小型奥运会——上，我见到的最高个子是一位六英尺十寸、三百二十磅的俄罗斯人，名叫卢卡斯·拉库廷（Lukas Lakutsin）。他在芳丹宝（Fontainebleau）泳池边吃午饭，穿着一件背心，我当时正不太得体地盯着他前臂上的静脉看。我们握了手；那一刻我感觉自己像个初生孩童在抓着父亲。那块块的胸肌、那肩胛三角肌。我问他点了什么吃的，他回答说："都行"。

paragraphs.en: Lakutsin was in town to watch the competition; not everyone who was enhanced at the Enhanced Games was an athlete. The Games had a small slate of events: the hundred-metre dash, some swimming, and some weight lifting. Competitors could choose from a menu of performance-enhancing drugs, or P.E.D.s, which were banned in mainstream sports, as long as they had F.D.A. approval—anabolics, human growth hormone, stimulants. “Enhancement” was the organizers’ preferred euphemism for what the Olympics refers to, in its own loaded way, as “doping.” The term did have a liberating effect. I found myself asking everyone I encountered if he was enhanced. Lakutsin: enhanced, but only before bodybuilding competitions. A life coach from North Carolina, whose biceps I wanted to touch: enhanced. Fabio, a Michael Jackson look-alike in town for the event: not enhanced, unless you count the rhinoplasty. Only one guy, Timothy Piatkowski, a researcher involved with the Games, suggested that my question might be a little rude. “You wouldn’t necessarily ask someone so openly, ‘When did you inject meth and for how long?’ ” he said. Piatkowski was enhanced, though: “A whole bunch of androgens, growth promoters, peptides.”
paragraphs.zh: 拉库廷是来现场观赛的；并非所有“增强者”都参加了比赛。赛事项目很少：百米冲刺、一些游泳项目和举重。参赛者可以从一张“兴奋剂”名单里挑选被称为表现增强药物（P.E.D.）使用，正是这些药在主流体育里被禁止，但只要获得美国食品药品监督管理局（F.D.A.）批准就可以：包括合成代谢类固醇、生长激素、兴奋剂。组织方更喜欢用“增强”这个委婉说法，代替奥运会那套带有价值判断色彩的“兴奋剂”叫法。这个称呼确实有种解放人的感觉。我发现自己会问起每一个遇见的人：他是否“增强过”。拉库廷：是，但只在健美比赛前。来自北卡罗来纳的一位人生教练，看到他那对肱二头肌我很想去摸：是“增强”过的。法比奥（Fabio）是一位像迈克尔·杰克逊的同款造型，专程来参加活动：不是“增强”，除非把隆鼻手术算在里面。只有一位叫蒂莫西·皮亚特科夫斯基（Timothy Piatkowski）的研究员，参与了这次比赛的组织，建议我这问题或许有些无礼，“你可不该当面直接问：‘你什么时候打过冰毒、打了多久？’”皮亚特科夫斯基自己也是“增强”过的，“一大堆雄激素、促生长因子、肽类药物。”

paragraphs.en: I got into Las Vegas on a Wednesday, as the athletes were acclimating to the venue, a temporary twenty-five-hundred-seat arena outside Resorts World. I stumbled onto powerlifters grunting in a hotel ballroom. Ripped guys with perfectly formed butts and bulging shoulders were swimming laps in the pool. The inaugural Games would take place on Sunday. The gathering also served as a product launch and a medical convention. Enhanced Group, the company behind the Games, had recently begun selling peptides, supplements, and testosterone, over the counter and by prescription, to the public. It was conducting a study on the effects of the drugs on its athletes and was holding events earlier in the week to proclaim the benefits of enhancements for everybody.
paragraphs.zh: 我周三抵达拉斯维加斯时，运动员们正在适应场馆——一家名叫Resorts World外的临时两千五百座体育馆。我一头扎进了酒店宴会厅里正在咆哮的力量举运动员群。肌肉线条分明、臀部圆润紧实、肩膀鼓鼓发胀的壮汉们在泳池里做着来回划水。首届比赛将在周日举行。这次聚会同时也是产品发布会和医学研讨会。主办方 Enhanced Group 最近开始向公众销售肽类、营养补充剂和睾酮，既有非处方也有处方渠道。公司还在对其运动员进行药物效果研究，并在本周早些时候举办了一系列活动，向大家宣扬增强技术对每个人都有好处。

paragraphs.en: In mainstream sports, the aversion to P.E.D.s is so deeply ingrained that few people even discuss the topic. But athletes still use them. For obvious reasons, it’s difficult to know precisely how many are doing so, but a 2024 survey found that between six and nine per cent of élite athletes were doping. Most sports treat performance enhancers as they once did gambling: like an existential threat that undermines fairness and offends our shared humanity. Naturally, the International Olympic Committee wasn’t a fan of the Enhanced Games. World Aquatics, the body that oversees swimming, compared the athletes to “clowns juggling knives.” Richard Pound, the founding president of the World Anti-Doping Agency, or WADA, and a crusader against cheaters, told me, “Sport is essentially a form of social consensus as to what is O.K. and what is not O.K.” He went on, “You don’t start off in sport to become this artificial monster. You’ll see some of the folks at the Games. I think you’ll probably conclude that, if it’s not grotesque, it borders on it.” The leader of one sport federation predicted that, as a result of the Games, someone would die.
paragraphs.zh: 在主流体育界，对兴奋剂（P.E.D.）的厌恶已经根深蒂固，几乎很少有人公开讨论这个话题。然而运动员仍在使用它。由于原因显而易见，我们很难准确知道有多少人如此，但一项 2024 年的调查发现，六到九个百分点的顶尖运动员在服用兴奋剂。大多数运动项目把兴奋剂当成过去对赌博的处理方式：一种破坏公平、冒犯人类共同尊严的存亡威胁。国际奥林匹克委员会自然也不喜欢“增强者运动会”。管理游泳的世界游泳联合会把参赛者比作“扔刀子的马戏小丑”。世界反兴奋剂机构（WADA）创始主席、反对作弊的斗士理查德·鲍姆曾对我说：“体育本质上是一种社会共识，关于什么可以、什么不可以。”他接着说：“你不会一开始参加比赛就是为了变成这种人工怪物。你会在这些比赛中看到一些人。我想你可能会认为，这样的人虽然不至于荒诞，却几乎就在那里。”一位体育联盟负责人预测，正是因为这项赛事，结果会有人丧命。

paragraphs.en: At Resorts World, no one seemed too worried. I saw enhanced men and women tooling through the casino on mobility scooters or sipping coffee in the rooftop pool. Large men delighted in meeting other large men. Lakutsin told me the gathering was affirming of humanity. “In Russia, people watching you, and they thinking you look like a freak,” he said. “If you do not look like other people, they start hate you.” At the food court, he’d run into Mitchell Hooper, the reigning World’s Strongest Man, who was in the dead-lift competition. “He says, ‘What the fuck! You’re so huge!’ ” Lakutsin recalled. Some Enhanced employees were also enhanced. In the mornings, the hotel gym was packed with wired, shiny-skinned guys grunting by the squat racks, making the hungover treadmill joggers and stair steppers self-conscious.
paragraphs.zh: 在世界度假村（Resorts World）里，没有人似乎太担心。我看到一些“增强者”男女乘着代步车穿行于赌场，或在屋顶泳池边喝咖啡。健壮的大块头们乐于与其他大块头见面。拉库金斯（Lakutsin）对我说，这次聚会让人重拾了人性。“在俄罗斯，总有人看着你，觉得你像个怪物。”他这么说。“如果你看起来不像其他人，他们就开始憎恨你。”在美食广场里，他遇见了身为“世界最强壮者”现任冠军的米切尔·胡珀（Mitchell Hooper），对方正在参加硬拉比赛。拉库金斯回忆道：“他说：‘他妈的，你也太壮了吧！’”一些增强项目的工作人员本身也是增强者。清晨，酒店健身房里挤满了通电般紧绷、皮肤闪亮的男士，他们在深蹲架前咕噜叫喊，让那些宿醉后在跑步机上慢跑和踩踏步机的人都变得很局促。

paragraphs.en: One problem with anti-doping enforcement is that it’s been impossible to define what doping is. Athletes are people who seek physical enhancement, in one form or another. They practice. They train. This self-improvement has always included the intake of various substances: animal testicles, raw eggs, Gatorade. One Olympic swimming team in the nineteen-seventies is said to have considered administering helium enemas, for buoyancy. Cyclists have tried strychnine. Marathoners have drunk brandy. It turned out that most things other than water, protein, and some salts and sugar were performance impairers. Only recently have we developed drugs for athletes that work.
paragraphs.zh: 反兴奋剂执法的一个问题是，长期以来我们几乎无法定义什么叫“兴奋剂”。运动员是追求体能提升的人，只是形式不同而已。他们会练习、会训练。这种自我强化始终包含摄入各种物质：动物睾丸、鸡蛋蛋白、佳得乐（Gatorade）。据说上世纪七十年代有一支奥运游泳队曾考虑过使用氦气灌肛，借此提高浮力。骑行运动员试过士的宁（Strychnine）。马拉松运动员则喝白兰地。结果发现，除水、蛋白质以及一些盐和糖以外，大多数东西都会削弱表现。只有最近，我们才真正开发出对运动员有效的兴奋药物。

paragraphs.en: At first, most sports allowed the drugs. Our attitude toward P.E.D.s has largely tracked public sentiment toward Big Pharma. In 1968, in response to a few athlete deaths that were believed to be linked to P.E.D. use, the Olympics began drug testing. The Olympic Committee introduced a definition of doping: the use of foreign substances in abnormal quantities to enhance performance. There were problems with this. It could be read, for example, to outlaw an excess of sugary snacks during a bike race. The drug tests were largely symbolic. Worldwide, about two Olympic athletes a year tested positive. When Mark McGwire broke the home-run record, in 1998, he kept a bottle of androstenedione in his locker. Major League Baseball had banned steroids, but it didn’t test for them. Perhaps what did the most to turn the public against steroids was the sense that some athletes were breaking the rules and getting away with it. WADA was founded in 1999. Pound, the founding president, told me that there weren’t lengthy discussions about why a given substance should be banned. WADA tried a new definition of doping. Using a substance is cheating if it meets two of three criteria: it enhances performance, it represents a health risk, and it violates “the spirit of sport.”
paragraphs.zh: 起初，大多数运动项目都允许使用这些药物。我们对 P.E.D. 的态度在很大程度上跟公众对大型制药公司的态度同步。1968 年，因若干被认为与兴奋剂使用有关的运动员死亡事件，奥运会开始进行药检。奥委会当时给出了一种兴奋剂定义：使用过量异物以增强表现。这个定义有问题。比如它可以被解读为禁止在自行车赛期间过量食用含糖零食。那时的药检几乎带有象征性质。全球每年约有两名奥运运动员检出阳性。1998 年，马克·麦格韦（Mark McGwire）打破全垒打纪录时，在储物柜里放着一瓶雄烯二酮（androstenedione）。美国职业棒球大联盟早已禁止类固醇，但并未对其进行检测。也许更让公众反感激素类药物的，是一种感觉：有些运动员明明违规则还能逍遥法外。WADA 于 1999 年成立。创始主席鲍姆告诉我，当初并没有就某种物质为何应被禁令化进行长篇讨论。WADA 尝试采用新的兴奋剂定义。若某种物质满足以下三项中的两项，就算作弊：它提高了成绩、它对健康有风险、以及它违反了“体育精神”。

paragraphs.en: According to WADA, the spirit of sport is “the celebration of the human spirit, body and mind.” Pound told me, “It’s a little fuzzy, but at least it gives you the warm feeling about what sport should be.” The fuzziness, perhaps, allows for the fact that we’re all enhanced in one way or another: Viagra, smartphones, Lasik, Claude. We eat enriched grains and receive flu vaccines. Some wiggle room helps. But WADA’s rules can be arbitrary: huffing supplemental oxygen (performance enhancing, but not harmful) doesn’t violate the spirit of sport. Caffeine (performance enhancing and, like almost anything, a little bit harmful) once violated the spirit of sport, but now only extremely high levels of it do. The concept of doping isn’t much of a thing in pursuits other than sports. Actors use Botox and hair plugs to stay camera-ready. Pianists with stagefright take beta-blockers. You could say John Lennon was doping when he dropped acid and wrote “I Am the Walrus,” but you wouldn’t. The invocation of “spirit” seems to assert that sports have a special claim on the soul.
paragraphs.zh: 根据世界反兴奋剂机构（WADA）的定义，体育精神是“对人类精神、身体和头脑的庆祝”。Pound跟我说：“这个定义有点模糊，但至少它能让你对体育应该是什么样子这种想法有一种温暖的感觉。”这种模糊性，也许反映了我们每个人在某种程度上都在增强：从伟哥、智能手机、激光近视手术到Claude。我们吃强化谷物、打流感疫苗。适当的“回旋余地”很有帮助。但WADA的规则也可能是任意的：吸入辅助氧气（能提升表现，但不有害）并不违反体育精神。咖啡因（能提升表现，而且像几乎所有东西一样有点有害）过去曾违反体育精神，现在只有极高剂量才会被视为违规。兴奋剂这个概念在体育之外并不适用。演员会打玻尿酸、种植发梢来保持上镜状态。患有怯场恐惧的钢琴家会服用β受体阻滞剂。你可以说约翰·列侬在吸食迷幻剂并写下《I Am the Walrus》时是在“兴奋剂”状态下，但你通常不会这样说。提及“精神”仿佛是在宣称，体育拥有一种特别的灵魂主张。

paragraphs.en: But the human spirit is a tricky thing. We cheat on our taxes, and we round up our height. N.B.A. players flop. Alva Noë, a philosopher at Berkeley, has said that, though doping is technically cheating, in that it’s against the rules, the doper isn’t much different from the basketball player who commits a rough foul. “It is cheating that is consistent, in so many ways, with the spirit of self-actualization, creativity, and achievement that is the mark of all sports,” he wrote. “To be willing to give your all even at the risk of injury to yourself and to others is actually what is required of any player who aims at excellence.” When I talked to Noë about the Enhanced Games, he said, “It poses an interesting question about our conception of what it is to be a human agent. Maybe a more honest way to understand human genius is to realize that we’re very dependent on others and on technology.” He continued, “When do I forfeit my ownership of my own accomplishments because of something I ate or took? I think that’s just a really open question.”
paragraphs.zh: 但人的精神并不简单。我们会逃税，也会把身高报高。NBA球员会故意“装摔”。伯克利大学哲学家阿尔瓦·诺厄（Alva Noë）曾说，虽然从技术上讲兴奋剂是作弊，因为它违反规则，但使用兴奋剂的人与在篮球比赛中犯故意犯规的球员并没有太大不同。“这是一种与体育标志性的自我实现、创造力和成就精神在许多方面都一致的作弊，”他写道。“愿意全力以赴，即使这会使自己和他人受伤，这实际上正是任何追求卓越的运动员所必须具备的。”当我和诺厄聊到“增强者竞赛（Enhanced Games）”时，他说：“它提出了一个有趣问题：我们如何理解人的行动者身份。也许更诚实地理解人类天赋的方式，是意识到我们对他人和技术都非常依赖。”他接着说：“如果我吃了或服用了某样东西，就要因此放弃对自己成绩归属权吗？我想这确实是一个很开放的问题。”

paragraphs.en: Aron D’Souza, a founder of the Enhanced Games, is friends with Sam Altman, the C.E.O. of OpenAI. Altman once described D’Souza as “ruthlessly ambitious,” a “monster networker,” and “obsessed with status and power in a way that is hard for me to relate to.” In 2009, when D’Souza was a student at Oxford, the venture capitalist Peter Thiel gave a guest lecture. D’Souza managed to arrange a meeting. Thiel, who has a passion for immortality, introduced himself to D’Souza by saying, “Hi, I’m Peter, and I’m going to live forever.” D’Souza recalled to the Financial Times, “I think I just replied, ‘Hi, I’m Aron. I’m studying law.’ ” D’Souza schmoozed. On a walk around campus, he asked Thiel if he had any nagging problems. Thiel mentioned the news outlet Gawker, which had recently outed him as gay. A year later, D’Souza devised and orchestrated a plan in which Thiel secretly funnelled millions to the wrestler Hulk Hogan so that he would wage a defamation lawsuit against Gawker. Hogan won a hundred-and-forty-million-dollar judgment. Gawker filed for bankruptcy.
paragraphs.zh: 增强者竞赛的创始人阿隆·D’Souza（Aron D’Souza）是OpenAI首席执行官萨姆·奥特曼（Sam Altman）的朋友。奥特曼曾形容D’Souza“冷酷而有野心”，是个“网络关系经营高手”，而且“对地位和权力有一种让我很难产生共鸣的执着”。2009年，D’Souza还是牛津大学学生时，风险投资人彼得·蒂尔（Peter Thiel）做客讲座。D’Souza成功安排了一次会面。蒂尔是个痴迷永生的人，他向D’Souza自我介绍时说：“嗨，我叫彼得，我打算永生不死。”D’Souza在《金融时报》回忆道：“我想我只是回了一句，‘嗨，我是阿隆。我在学习法律。’”随后D’Souza便开始频繁接触（攀关系）。在一次校园散步中，他问蒂尔是否有烦人的问题。蒂尔提到了新闻机构Gawker，因为它最近公开“出柜”他是同性恋。一年后，D’Souza设计并策划了一个计划：让蒂尔暗中向摔跤手霍克·霍根（Hulk Hogan）输送数千万美元，以便霍根提起对Gawker的诽谤诉讼。霍根赢得了一亿四千万美元的判决，Gawker随后申请了破产。

paragraphs.en: D’Souza established himself in Thiel’s inner circle, which comprised mostly men, most of whom were gay and most of whom were tech founders or venture capitalists. They held dinners and vacationed together. Some were transhumanists. In 2022, D’Souza was in Miami, where Thiel was hosting a New Year’s party. At a gym, he encountered an unusually muscled lifter. The guy told D’Souza that he was on testosterone. During the next few days, D’Souza worked up a plan for the Enhanced Games. At the New Year’s party, he pitched Thiel, who later invested. (Eventually, so did the Winklevoss twins.) A German billionaire named Christian Angermayer, another member of Thiel’s circle, became a co-founder.
paragraphs.zh: D’Souza在蒂尔的核心圈子里站稳了脚跟，这个圈子主要是男性成员，其中大多数是同性恋者，也大多是科技创业者或风险投资家。他们会聚餐、一起度假。有人是跨人类主义者。2022年，D’Souza在迈阿密，当时蒂尔在那里主办跨年派对。在一家健身房，他遇到了一位肌肉格外发达的举重者。那人告诉D’Souza自己正在注射睾酮。接下来的几天里，D’Souza就制定了增强者竞赛的方案。在跨年派对上，他向蒂尔推介了这个想法，后来蒂尔也投了资。（后来，温克莱沃斯双胞胎（Winklevoss twins）也加入了投资。）蒂尔圈子里的另一名成员、德国亿万富翁克里斯蒂安·安格尔迈耶（Christian Angermayer）也成为了联合创始人。

paragraphs.en: D’Souza launched the Enhanced Games in 2023, with a trollish promotional video that aped the language of gay liberation. A voice-over announced, “I am a proud enhanced athlete. The Olympics hate me. I need your help to come out. I need your help to stop hate.” In the media, the Enhanced Games became known as the Steroid Olympics. I first talked to D’Souza shortly afterward. He was thinking of inaugurating the Games during the Paris Olympics, as counterprogramming; the athletes could use any drug they wanted. The event didn’t come together, but his provocations got a lot of attention. He pitched Lance Armstrong on investing. Armstrong declined.
paragraphs.zh: 德索萨（D’Souza）于2023年启动了“增强者运动会”，并拍了一个带有挑衅意味的宣传视频，模仿了“同性恋解放”中的说辞。画外音宣称：“我是一个自豪的增强型运动员。奥林匹克不喜欢我。我需要你们帮助我出柜。我需要你们帮助我阻止仇恨。”在媒体中，“增强者运动会”很快被称为“类固醇奥运会”。我随后不久就与德索萨进行过交谈。他当时正考虑在巴黎奥运会期间，以“对向节目”方式启动这项赛事，让运动员可使用任何想要的药物。这项赛事最终未能成行，但他的挑衅行为获得了大量关注。他向兰斯·阿姆斯特朗（Lance Armstrong）推介了这项投资提议，但后者予以拒绝。

paragraphs.en: I was surprised to learn that D’Souza is not a sports fan. “I’m not the kind of guy who will, like, go to the football on a Saturday,” he told me. “I’m a philosopher. I read old printed books in my library at Oxford.” Angermayer also began following sports only recently. “It’s very hard to be a fan of anything if I’m not involved,” he told me. “Now that I’m able to play in that world, I’m super obsessed about it.” In 2024, D’Souza pitched the Games at an academic conference on doping in Denmark. His remarks were not what attendees expected. “He did not talk so much about sport,” Verner Møller, who organized the conference, told me. “He was talking about a future where we would not die because we have found medicine that would allow us to live eternally.” D’Souza told me that the Games were a proof of concept for a larger mission: “My thesis has always been, with the Enhanced Games, it works, we generate revenue, we can push that into scientific development, better drugs come out, and eventually, through capitalism, we cure death.”
paragraphs.zh: 我很意外地得知德索萨并不是一个体育迷。“我不是那种会在周六去看足球的人，”他对我说。“我更像个哲学家。我在牛津的图书馆里读旧版印刷书籍。”安格马耶（Angermayer）直到最近才开始关注体育。“如果自己没有参与进去，要成为任何事物的粉丝都很难，”他对我说。“现在我能进入那个世界了，我几乎是被它迷得神魂颠倒。”2024年，德索萨在丹麦的一场关于兴奋剂的学术会议上推介了这项赛事。他的发言并非听众所预期的那样。“他几乎没有谈运动，”组织会议的维纳·穆勒（Verner Møller）告诉我。“他谈的是一个未来：我们不会死，因为我们找到了能让人类永生的药物。”德索萨对我说，这些运动会是他更宏大目标的概念验证：“我的论点一直是，通过增强者运动会，它能运转，我们能产生收入，然后把这些收入投入到科学研发，研发出更好的药物，最终在资本主义体制下，我们治愈死亡。”

paragraphs.en: In early 2024, Angermayer published an essay titled “The Future Is Enhanced,” a manifesto predicting an era of science-aided human improvement which would arrive in waves: sexual enhancement, muscular enhancement, cognitive enhancement, happiness enhancement, and a final, transhumanist wave involving brain-computer interfaces. Angermayer calls this humanmaxxing. He rejects the idea of living forever—“Statistically, you’re going to have an accident, and, if you don’t have an accident, then someday the star system will end”—though he does think that we are a matter of years away from achieving indefinite life spans.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年初，安格马耶发表了一篇题为《未来已增强》的论文，宣称未来将进入科学辅助的人类增强时代，并会分阶段到来：性能力增强、肌肉增强、认知增强、幸福感增强，最后是涉及脑机接口的超人类主义浪潮。安格马耶将其称为“humanmaxxing”。他否认“永生”这一想法——“从统计上看，你迟早会遇到一次事故；如果没出事故，那么总有一天你的恒星系统会结束”——但他确实认为我们距离实现理论上无限寿命只差几年。

paragraphs.en: Angermayer runs Apeiron Investment Group, which has invested heavily in biotech and longevity. Its companies research therapeutic psychedelics and treatments for premature ejaculation. One afternoon in Las Vegas, he invited me up to his hotel suite. When I arrived, he was nibbling on nigiri. He is forty-eight and makes intense eye contact. I couldn’t detect a single pore in his face. Angermayer told me that he takes the peptides GLP-1 (for weight maintenance), tesamorelin (for growth-hormone production), and GHK-Cu (for skin regeneration); the anti-aging hormone DHEA; and a number of sleep supplements. He has been on testosterone-replacement therapy since his thirties. On busy days, he takes modafinil, a wakefulness drug used to treat narcolepsy. This week, leading up to the Games, there were many busy days.
paragraphs.zh: 安格马耶经营着 Apeiron Investment Group，这家公司对生物科技和长寿领域投入巨大。旗下企业研究治疗性迷幻药以及治疗早泄的疗法。2024年某个下午，他邀请我到拉斯维加斯的酒店套房拜访。我到达时，他正咬着一口握寿司。现年四十八岁，他的目光凝视得很有穿透力，我看不出他脸上有半点毛孔。安格马耶告诉我，他使用 GLP-1（用于体重维持）、tesamorelin（用于促进生长激素生成）和 GHK-Cu（用于皮肤再生）；还有抗衰老激素 DHEA，以及多种助眠补充剂。他从三十多岁起就开始了睾酮替代治疗。工作繁忙的日子里，他会服用莫达非尼，这是一种用于治疗发作性睡病的警觉性促进药。就在本周、在比赛临近之际，他度过了许多繁忙的日子。

paragraphs.en: Enhanced’s drug study, which will track the athletes over five years, was preparing to release early data, and Angermayer was excited. “We’re going to change the debate, and then the debate will be: Is it actually morally acceptable that we’re not allowing these drugs?” he said.
paragraphs.zh: Enhanced 的药物研究计划将持续追踪运动员五年，正在准备发布早期数据，Angermayer 对此感到兴奋。“我们将改变这场争论，而之后的争论会是：我们不允许使用这些药物是否真的符合道德？”他如是说。

paragraphs.en: Angermayer had pushed D’Souza to moderate his initial anything-goes instincts. Enhanced now encourages the use only of drugs that have F.D.A. approval. “They’re the best agency in the world to decide what should be in the human body or not,” he said. “I’m not the arbiter—nor should WADA be.” Enhanced says it tests for health, not for drugs. Athletes with unsafe levels of certain biomarkers wouldn’t be allowed to compete, regardless of how they got that way. WADA, which views taking P.E.D.s, safe or not, as cheating, rejects the approach. But it’s popular among philosophers. Peter Singer is a fan.
paragraphs.zh: Angermayer 曾劝 D’Souza 收敛最初那种‘想用什么就用什么’的本能。如今 Enhanced 只鼓励使用经过美国食品药品监督管理局（F.D.A.）批准的药物。“他们是世界上最适合裁定人体内该有、该没有什么东西的机构，”他说，“我不是裁定者——WADA 也不应该是。”Enhanced 说它是为了监测健康而不是监测药物。无论运动员如何获得，若某些生物标志物达到危险水平，他们就不能参赛。WADA 将使用兴奋性药物（P.E.D.），不论安全与否，都视为作弊，因此拒绝这种做法。但这一思路在哲学家中颇受欢迎。彼得·辛格就是其中一位支持者。

paragraphs.en: I told Angermayer that I thought his approach was rational, but that enjoyment of sports was an irrational endeavor. (Peter Singer told me that he was intrigued by the Games intellectually but added, “It’s not something that I’m going to say, ‘Oh, great, the Enhanced Games are on! I must make sure that I can watch.’ ”) Angermayer asked, “Why do you think we like sports? Have you ever thought about that?”
paragraphs.zh: 我告诉 Angermayer，我认为他的方法是理性的，但体育运动本身的享受却是一种非理性行为。（彼得·辛格告诉我，他在智识层面对这些比赛很感兴趣，但也补充道：‘这不是那种我会说，哦，太好了，Enhanced Games 开赛了！我一定要确保自己能看上。’）Angermayer 问道：‘你认为我们为何热爱体育？你是否想过这个问题？’

paragraphs.en: I said I thought that most people watch sports, basically, to feel something.
paragraphs.zh: 我回答说，我觉得大多数人观看体育比赛，基本上都是为了感受某种情绪。

paragraphs.en: “If you look at it scientifically, we are actually evolutionarily primed for comparing our skills,” he replied. “Go to the zoo and watch gorillas, or any form of apes. They keep that raw display of who’s the strongest, who’s the fastest.” When Enhanced athletes proved to be faster and stronger, why would anyone watch anything else? This put pressure on the Enhanced Games. “For it to be a success, there need to be world records,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: “如果从科学角度看，我们在进化上其实天生就倾向于比较彼此的能力，”他回答道。“去动物园看看大猩猩，或者任何类人猿。它们都在进行那种原始的比拼：谁更强、谁更快。”当增强运动员证明自己又更快又更强后，谁还愿意看别的东西？这给增强运动会施加了压力。“要想成功，就必须有世界纪录，”他说。

paragraphs.en: The next morning, I caught a training session at the pool. It was difficult to discern the outward effects of enhancement on the athletes. The swimmers looked like swimmers—sinewy and long, with huge hands and feet. An exception was James Magnussen, an Australian who’d been the first person to sign up with Enhanced and had promised to “juice to the gills.” In an attempt, last year, at a world record, he’d put on too much muscle and had basically sunk. He’d slimmed down since then, but, as he swam laps, his lats still oozed out of the sides of his swimsuit. One person commented online, “You can see his back from his front.”
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早晨，我在泳池里赶上了一次训练。要分辨这些运动员外表上是否有强化增强的影响并不容易。游泳运动员看起来就像游泳运动员——肌肉紧实又修长，手和脚也都很大。例外是詹姆斯·马格努森（James Magnussen），一名澳大利亚人，他是第一个与“增强”签约的运动员，并且曾承诺要“全力补给、猛灌兴奋剂”。去年在一次世界纪录尝试中，他长得太多肌肉，基本上沉了下去。此后他瘦了不少，但当他划水训练时，他的背阔肌仍从泳衣两侧鼓胀鼓起。有人在网上评论说：“你从正面都能看见他的背。”

paragraphs.en: Most of the athletes were Olympians who’d retired or were at the back ends of their careers. A few were serving WADA bans. A small number were competing clean. A clean swimmer named Hunter Armstrong told me that his decision to forgo P.E.D.s was about celebrating the talent given to him by God. Also, he wanted to maintain his Olympics eligibility. He was trailed all week by anti-doping officials, who took urine and blood samples. Money was a major draw for everyone there. Enhanced paid the athletes salaries, and each winner would get a quarter-million-dollar bonus. A world record would pay an extra million. After the swimmers finished up for the day, I met with Megan Romano, a former world-champion swimmer who’d retired ten years ago. She was now thirty-five and living in Las Vegas, where she made money doing odd jobs, betting on sports, and working loopholes at the slot machines. Romano had no realistic shot at a world record, but Enhanced was hoping she’d demonstrate that drugs could shave years off your biological age. Romano hadn’t trained for a decade. She’d done some hard living. She told me, “It’s crazy, because the warmup pool that we’re using, it’s at Ayu,” one of the hotel’s day-party clubs. “A year ago, I was partying in that pool.”
paragraphs.zh: 大多数运动员都是已经退役或职业生涯后期的奥运选手。其中一些人还在接受 WADA 的禁令。少量运动员是“纯净参赛”的。一个名叫亨特·阿姆斯特朗（Hunter Armstrong）的“纯净”游泳运动员告诉我，他放弃使用 P.E.D.（禁用提升类药物）是为了庆祝上天赐予他的天赋。此外，他还想保持奥运会参赛资格。这周里他一直被反兴奋剂官员跟踪，官员们抽取了他的尿液和血液样本。金钱对所有人都是主要吸引力。增强项目给运动员发放工资，每个获胜者还能拿到二十五万美元奖金。若创造世界纪录，还能再赢一百万。游泳队在当日结束训练后，我与梅根·罗曼诺（Megan Romano）会面。她是前世界冠军游泳运动员，十年前退役。现在三十五岁，住在拉斯维加斯，靠做零工、赌球和在老虎机上“钻漏洞”挣钱。罗曼诺对打破世界纪录几乎没有现实机会，但增强团队希望她能证明，药物能够让你的生物年龄倒退许多年。罗曼诺已经十年没训练了。她的生活也并不轻松。她对我说：“这真是太疯狂了，因为我们现在用来热身的泳池，在阿尤（Ayu）——这是酒店里的日间派对俱乐部。去年的同一时候，我在那个泳池里玩得很疯。”

paragraphs.en: Angermayer’s manifesto had credited transgender activism with inspiring Enhanced. “The trans movement will ultimately extend to the ability to shape and enhance one’s own body and mind according to individual desires and aspirations,” he wrote. But the Games didn’t have any trans athletes. The competition categories were not men and women but, rather, XX and XY chromosome groups. Angermayer, who as a young man had hidden that he was gay, told me, “If you’re trans, do you really need to go into sports? Maybe that’s your passion, but don’t you have, also, other passions? And is it worth it? It’s at least what I asked myself: Is it worth it to shout out from the rooftops that I am gay? No fucking not! My life would be destroyed for nothing.”
paragraphs.zh: 安格迈尔在《宣言》中把跨性别维权运动称为对“增强”有启发的力量来源。“跨性别运动最终会延伸到按个人欲望和抱负塑造和增强自身身体与心智的能力，”他写道。但这个赛事没有任何跨性别运动员。比赛分类不是“男子”和“女子”，而是 XX 与 XY 染色体分组。安格迈尔年轻时曾把自己是同性恋的身份隐藏起来，他对我说：“如果你是跨性别，你真的需要进体育界吗？也许这是你的热情，但你还有别的热情吗？而且值得吗？我至少会问自己：这到底值得吗，要不要从屋顶大喊‘我是同性恋’？他妈的，绝对不值得！那会让我的人生毁于一旦。”

paragraphs.en: Before the Games, Enhanced had put its athletes in a fifteen-week performance-enhanced training camp at a resort in Abu Dhabi. (The Emiratis had agreed to regulate the drug study.) The participants underwent a battery of physical exams, and then were put on personalized protocols, prescribed by doctors, before receiving their first pills and injections. “That first day on the protocol was the hardest,” Ben Proud, a British freestyle swimmer who won a silver medal in Paris, said, after a practice. “I was terrified, because, to me, that was the day that I went from the Ben Proud that I always knew to a new person.” I’d spoken with Boady Santavy, a Canadian weight lifter, when he was three weeks into the protocol. He’d noticed that his sleep had improved, and that his recovery after training sessions was faster. Nonathletes often conceive of performance enhancers as the lazy option, but it’s typically the opposite: athletes take them so they can train even more. Santavy had already put on bulk. “They’re more full and thick,” he told me, of his muscles. He thought he might be able to put up a world-record weight already.
paragraphs.zh: 在奥运会之前，Enhanced把运动员送进了阿布扎比一家度假村里为期十五周的强化训练营。（埃米尔人同意对这项药物研究进行监管。）参与者先接受了大量体检，然后在医生开具的个性化方案下，才开始服用第一批药丸和注射剂。前来训练后，英国自由泳运动员本·普劳德（Ben Proud）说：那是协议第一天最难的日子。“我当时非常害怕，因为对我来说，那是我从一直以来的本·普劳德变成一个新人的那一天。”我在增强方案进行三周时采访了加拿大举重运动员波迪·桑塔维（Boady Santavy）。他注意到自己睡眠有所改善，训练后恢复也更快。非运动员常常把兴奋剂之类的成绩增强手段看作懒人选项，但通常恰恰相反：运动员使用它们是为了能训练得更多。桑塔维已经明显增肌了。“我的肌肉更结实、更丰满，”他告诉我。“我觉得自己可能已经能扛起一项世界纪录级重量了。”

paragraphs.en: Max Martin, Enhanced’s twenty-nine-year-old C.E.O., would not disclose his personal enhancement protocol but explained that he takes six times the dosage of some of the athletes. (He has previously said that he takes testosterone. “A byproduct of taking testosterone is that it takes down your fertility,” he told GQ. “I’m not at a point in my life where I want to have kids, so actually taking down my fertility is a great thing.”) Mitchell Hooper, the deadlifter, said that he was taking testosterone, Anadrol, nandrolone, and Halotestin—all anabolic steroids—plus Adderall on competition days, for focus. Hooper, who set his own protocol and wasn’t part of Enhanced’s study, said he used to take higher doses. He’d sometimes found himself crying inexplicably at his kitchen counter from the Adderall. “It’s a bad idea,” he said. “Perfect, optimal health right now? Don’t do them.” But, for him, the trade-off—money, a platform—was worth it.
paragraphs.zh: Enhanced公司29岁的首席执行官马克斯·马丁（Max Martin）拒绝披露自己的个人强化方案，但他解释说自己在一些运动员身上会使用六倍剂量。（他先前公开过自己在服用睾酮。“睾酮的一个副作用是会降低生育力，”他在接受《GQ》采访时说，“我现在的人生阶段并不想要孩子，所以把生育力降下来其实是件好事。”）硬拉冠军米切尔·胡珀（Mitchell Hooper）说，他在比赛日会服用睾酮、阿纳卓（Anadrol）、萘甲环酸（nandrolone）和哈洛替（Halotestin）——全部都是合成代谢类固醇——再加上安非他命类药物Adderall来集中注意力。胡珀自己制定方案，并未参与Enhanced的研究，他说自己以前曾用更高剂量。过去他有时会在厨房台面上无缘无故地哭泣，那是由Adderall引起的。“这不是好主意，”他说。“你现在要是想要最佳、健康状态？那就别用它们。”但对他来说，权衡之后——金钱、曝光平台——是值得的。

paragraphs.en: WADA was founded around the time of the war on drugs, and authorities warned that steroids would kill you. EPO, a naturally occurring hormone that is used by athletes to increase endurance, can dangerously thicken the blood. (WADA allows athletes to use non-pharmaceutical interventions, such as hypobaric chambers, to raise EPO levels.) Anabolic steroids can cause mood swings, shrunken testicles, baldness, and infertility, and can suppress the body’s ability to produce testosterone on its own. Heavy doses over a long time are linked to a higher likelihood of heart disease and stroke. But steroids aren’t going to kill you, at least not in the short or medium term. Norman Fost, a pediatrician and bioethicist at the University of Wisconsin, and a longtime opponent of the anti-doping regime, told me, “There’s literally a hundred times more documented deaths from playing football than from using steroids.”
paragraphs.zh: 世界反兴奋剂机构（WADA）成立于反毒战争时期，当时主管部门曾警告类固醇会要了你的命。红细胞生成素EPO是一种人体自然产生的激素，运动员用它来提高耐力，但它可能会让血液危险地变得过稠。（WADA允许运动员使用非药物干预手段，例如高压舱室训练，来提升EPO水平。）合成代谢类固醇可能引发情绪波动、睾丸萎缩、脱发以及不育，并能抑制人体自行产生睾酮的能力。长期、大剂量使用与心脏病和中风风险上升有关。但类固醇不会让你立刻死掉，至少从短期到中期不会。威斯康星大学小儿科医生兼生物伦理学家、反反兴奋剂制度长期反对者诺曼·福斯特（Norman Fost）告诉我：足球运动员死亡的文献记录数量“字面上高出一百倍”，而使用类固醇致死的记录却少得多。

paragraphs.en: Athletes use drugs because of financial pressure, because of injuries, because everyone else seems to be doing it. One afternoon, I talked with Marvin Bracy-Williams, a sprinter who signed with Enhanced after failing a drug test for steroids. Bracy-Williams told me that for the first ten years of his career he never considered doping. Then, in 2021, he injured his hamstring at the Olympic qualifiers. For years, he tried to regain his form. “I was doing everything, and still coming up short,” he said. “You start to have weak moments.” He began to feel like a sucker for competing clean. A friend of Bracy-Williams told the Guardian that the runner called him to vent. “Everything I’ve done, I’ve done the right way,” he said, according to the friend. “Dog, there ain’t no way these boys are that much better than me.” Bracy-Williams told me, “I took the opportunity, and then it bit me, so here we are.”
paragraphs.zh: 运动员使用药物，是因为金钱压力、因为伤病、因为觉得周围人都在这么做。一天午后，我与马文·布雷西-威廉姆斯（Marvin Bracy-Williams）交谈，他是在服用类固醇后药检不合格时才与Enhanced签约的短跑选手。布雷西-威廉姆斯告诉我，职业生涯前十年他从未认真考虑过兴奋剂。然后在2021年，他在奥运会资格赛中拉伤了腘绳肌。此后多年，他试图恢复状态。“我什么都在做，却仍然追不上标准，”他说。“你会开始有动摇的时候。”他逐渐觉得自己在“洁白竞争”中像个傻子。布雷西-威廉姆斯的一位朋友告诉《卫报》，这名跑者曾打电话向他发泄：“我做过的每一件事，都是按正确的方式去做。”朋友转述说，“兄弟，这群人不可能比我强太多。”布雷西-威廉姆斯对我说：“我抓住了机会，但后来它反噬了我，于是我们就来到了今天。”

paragraphs.en: Drugs help, but only on the margins. As skill increases, the effectiveness of P.E.D.s decreases. Any baseball player could take steroids—and thousands likely have—but none would be Barry Bonds. The big aid is in recovery and career longevity. The immediate effects are most pronounced in sports like weight lifting, cycling, and sprinting, where there is a more direct relationship between raw physical ability and results. Some sprinters believe that steroids can trim one stride, equal to about two-tenths of a second, off the hundred-metre dash. Of the top five fastest times in the dash, only one was set by a runner who never failed a drug test: Usain Bolt, who holds the record, at 9.58 seconds. The next closest time was 9.69—less than one stride slower. Bracy-Williams told me that Bolt’s run had moved him emotionally. “I’ve probably contributed a million views myself to the YouTube video of the world-record race, trying to figure out how,” he said. “It’s a work of art.” I asked him if he would be saddened if Bolt were revealed to have used steroids. “Think about it,” he said. “He still moved his body in 9.58 seconds, regardless of what was in there.”
paragraphs.zh: 药物有用，但仅仅是锦上添花。随着运动水平提升，类固醇的效果会下降。任何职业棒球运动员都可能使用类固醇——而且成千上万人很可能也用了——但没有一个是巴里·邦兹那样的。真正的大帮助在于恢复和职业寿命延长。类固醇的即时效果在举重、自行车和短跑这类项目里最明显，因为这些项目里运动成绩与原始体能的关系更直接。一些短跑运动员认为，类固醇可以把百米短跑缩短一个步幅，大约快两到三分之一秒中的两成（约0.2秒）。在百米最快的前五个成绩里，只有一个是从未被查出违禁药的运动员创造的：创造世界纪录的尤塞恩·博尔特，成绩是9.58秒。离他最近的是9.69秒，慢了不到一个步幅。布雷西-威廉姆斯告诉我，博尔特的那次奔跑让我产生了情感上的震撼。“我自己大概也给那场世界纪录赛的YouTube视频贡献了上百万次观看，一直在研究他是怎么做到的，”他说，“那是一件艺术品。”我问他，如果有证据表明博尔特使用了类固醇，他会不会感到失望。“想一想，”他说，“不管体内有什么，他还是在9.58秒里跑完了那段距离。”

paragraphs.en: On the morning of the Games, I met Aron D’Souza in the lobby of his hotel. He was feeling triumphant. “My advice to entrepreneurs is, like, You can just do things,” he said. Last year, he’d stepped down as C.E.O. He said he wanted to focus on new things. During New York’s mayoral election, he’d spoken to Andrew Cuomo’s team, to float his name for sanitation commissioner. “If I could have two years, I could clean up all the trash,” he told me. (He envisioned street-sweeping drones.)
paragraphs.zh: 比赛当天早晨，我在他酒店大堂见到阿龙·德索萨（Aron D’Souza）。他看起来很得意。“我给企业家的建议是，像样地去做事就行，”他说。去年，他卸任了首席执行官职位，表示想把精力放在新的事情上。在纽约市长选举期间，他曾与安德鲁·科莫（Andrew Cuomo）阵营的人会面，试探性地提名自己担任清洁事业专员的可能性。“如果我有两年的时间，我就能清理掉所有垃圾，”他对我说。（他设想的是用清扫街道的无人机。）

paragraphs.en: Almost reflexively, I asked whether he was enhanced. “Not presently, but I feel like I should be,” D’Souza said. He explained that, though he wasn’t enhanced pharmaceutically, he did follow some of the teachings of Bryan Johnson, the longevity-obsessed tech founder, who strictly limits his sun exposure, gets transfusions of his son’s blood plasma, and scrupulously logs his nighttime erections. (After the Games, D’Souza reported that he’d begun taking an enhancement, but he wouldn’t reveal what it was.)
paragraphs.zh: 我几乎是本能地问他是否在用增强剂。“现在没有，但我觉得我应该用，”德索萨说。他解释说，虽然他没有通过药物进行增强，但他确实遵循了极度追求长寿的科技创业者布莱恩·约翰逊（Bryan Johnson）的一些做法。约翰逊严格限制日晒、进行儿子血浆输注，并且仔细记录自己的夜间勃起情况。 （赛后，德索萨表示他已经开始服用一种增强剂，但他没有透露是什么。）

paragraphs.en: D’Souza told me that the opening of the Games was a landmark for humankind. “People see this as the start of the biotech salvationist movement,” he said. We talked about the ways in which the world might orient itself toward an enhanced era of indefinite life spans (we’d need to rejigger social security and pensions), but discussing eternal life at the Enhanced Games felt, at times, like discussing reaching low Earth orbit at the pole-vaulting championships. D’Souza had appeared on Joe Rogan’s podcast and compared the Games to the Apollo program. “You’re not going to the moon,” Rogan replied. “You’re just getting a bunch of guys juiced up running really fast.”
paragraphs.zh: 德索萨告诉我，比赛开幕是人类历史上的一个里程碑。“人们把这看作是生物技术救世主义运动的开端，”他说。我们讨论了世界可能如何转向一个寿命无限延长的增强时代（我们会需要重新调整社保和退休金体系），但在“增强者运动会”这个场景下谈论永生，有时听起来有点像在撑杆跳高比赛上聊“如何进入低地球轨道”。德索萨曾出现在乔·罗根（Joe Rogan）的播客里，并将这次比赛比作阿波罗计划。罗根回应说：“你不是在去登月。” “你只是让一群家伙把身体‘打鸡血’，然后跑得很快。”

paragraphs.en: D’Souza’s vision was that each annual Games would bring incremental advancements. “This is the first threshold of enhancement,” he said. The second threshold might include bionics. The third level would include brain-computer interfaces—“integrating physical intelligence,” D’Souza said. An athlete might be able to download new competencies: Michael Phelps’s swimming mechanics, Steph Curry’s jump shot.
paragraphs.zh: 德索萨认为，每届年度赛事都会带来渐进式的提升。“这是第一层增强门槛，”他表示。第二个层次可能包括义肢。第三个层次将涵盖脑机接口——“把身体智能整合进去，”德索萨说。运动员可能能够直接“下载”新的能力，比如迈克尔·菲尔普斯的游泳技术、斯蒂芬·库里的跳投技巧。

paragraphs.en: D’Souza said that Enhanced would draw a line somewhere. No jet packs to zoom down the track. I wondered how he’d avoid re-creating WADA’s definition problem. “The end conclusion of this project may be that Enhanced becomes the definitional regulator of what it means to be human,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 德索萨说，“增强”会在某个地方划出底线。比如不会允许穿喷气背包在赛道上冲刺。我不禁想，他如何避免重现世界反兴奋剂机构（WADA）定义问题的困境。“这个项目的最终结论可能是，Enhanced成为‘人类’到底意味着什么的定义性监管者，”他说。

paragraphs.en: The Games kicked off in the mid-afternoon. The gold Trump International Hotel loomed over the arena. The venture-capital firm of Donald Trump, Jr., was an investor, but the event conflicted with his wedding, in the Bahamas. (Angermayer told me that the Trump Administration’s friendly stance toward alternative medicines—psychedelics, peptides––had been a boon for the enhanced movement: “Trump winning was definitely a decisive factor in holding the Games here.”) At one end of the pool was a desk for the television commentators. Bryan Johnson had signed up to do the color commentary. The temperature was in the mid-nineties. Johnson held an umbrella and had on sunglasses that looked like the goggles scientists once wore during nuclear tests. Tickets weren’t for sale. Instead, Enhanced had invited a battery of influencers. Martin, the C.E.O., explained, “We’re built for social media, not television.” Lakutsin, the large Russian man, had come with Zoop, a social-media network created by a founding executive of OnlyFans, which had partnered with Enhanced. In the concourse, content creators filmed other content creators creating content. There was free food and drinks at kiosks labelled “+SOLIDS” and “+LIQUIDS.” Perhaps because of the filming frenzy in the concourse, or the transgressive aim of the competition, an air of permissiveness presided. I cut the +SOLIDS line and snagged a Wagyu slider.
paragraphs.zh: 比赛于下午中段开始。金色的特朗普国际酒店高耸在竞技场上方。唐纳德·特朗普之子旗下的风险投资公司是投资者之一，但活动时间与他在巴哈马的婚礼冲突。（安格迈尔告诉我，特朗普政府对替代医学（致幻剂、肽类）友好的态度，确实成了增强运动的一大助力：“特朗普胜选绝对是把比赛留在这里的决定性因素。”）泳池一端是电视解说席。布莱恩·约翰逊报名做了实况解说。气温在九十多度。约翰逊撑着伞，戴着看起来像科学家当年在核试验中戴过的护目镜。门票并不对外出售；取而代之的是，Enhanced邀请了一批网红。首席执行官马丁解释说：“我们是为社交媒体而生，而不是为了电视。”那位身材魁梧的俄罗斯男子拉库钦（Lakutsin）带来了Zoop——这是由OnlyFans的创始高管创建的一款社交网络，并与Enhanced合作。在休息廊道里，内容创作者正在拍摄其他内容创作者制作内容。标着“+SOLIDS”和“+LIQUIDS”的小亭子里有免费的食品和饮料。也许是由于廊道里的拍摄狂热，或比赛本身带有的越界氛围，现场弥漫着一种放任的气息。我绕开了+SOLIDS的队伍，抢到了一份和牛小汉堡。

paragraphs.en: The Games began. In the first event, a weight lifter named Beatriz Pirón just missed a world record in the snatch, in which a lifter pulls a barbell over her head in a single motion. But no one seemed stressed. The major record attempts would happen later. Hunter Armstrong, the clean swimmer, won the fifty-metre backstroke, but it wasn’t a record. After he was finished, he went off with anti-doping officials, to give more urine and blood.
paragraphs.zh: 比赛正式开始。第一项比赛中，举重运动员贝娅特丽克丝·皮龙（Beatriz Pirón）在“抓举”中仅差一点点就刷出世界纪录，抓举是举重者将杠铃一次性举过头顶的动作。可没有人显得紧张，重大纪录挑战会在后面出现。清洁泳手亨特·阿姆斯特朗赢得了五十米仰泳，但这不是纪录。在完成比赛后，他随即前往反兴奋剂官员处，继续提交更多的尿液和血液样本。

paragraphs.en: I was excited to see Santavy, the weight lifter, who had boasted to me that the world record in the snatch should be no problem. None of the Enhanced Game records would be recognized in mainstream sports, but, he told me, “I’ll consider mine the record for sure.” Enhanced was following standard competition rules: lifters had three chances to put up their best weight. Weight lifting is the sport where steroids should help the most: get stronger, lift heavier. But the other lifters kept failing. Santavy attempted the record on his second try, but he bailed out as the bar went overhead. He looked shocked. He had one more shot. The final attempt, though, wasn’t even close. He fell to the floor and slammed his fists on the mat. An announcer declared, “Ladies and gentleman, we have a surprise for you. We’re going to give Boady one! more! chance!” There was laughter in the arena. Santavy failed at that one, too.
paragraphs.zh: 我很兴奋地见到了举重运动员Santavy，他曾向我吹嘘抓举世界纪录对他来说完全不是问题。虽然任何“增强运动”里的纪录在主流体育中都不会被承认，但他说：我自己就把它当成纪录。Enhanced采用的是标准竞赛规则：举重者有三次机会来挑战最高重量。举重本来就是类固醇最该起作用的运动——更强、更能举起更重的重量。但是其他举重运动员都在不断失手。Santavy在第二次尝试中想突破纪录，却在杠铃举过头顶时停住了，直接放下。看得出他很震惊。因为他只剩最后一次机会。可是最后这次也差得很远。他倒在地上，把拳头重重拍在垫子上。解说员宣布：女士们先生们，我们给你们个惊喜，我们再给Boady一次！机会！现场响起了笑声。Santavy这次也失败了。

paragraphs.en: It was early, but the lack of records threatened disaster for Enhanced. If the drugs couldn’t help a lifter lift more, what was the upside for the rest of us? Interestingly, Santavy’s failure affirmed that you could stage a sporting event with drugs and not lose the drama. Pound had told me that performance enhancers ruined sports because they effectively fixed the results: “The key about sport is the inherent uncertainty of the outcome.” I wondered if WADA and Enhanced both overestimated how much the enhancements helped. Sports are fun because the better physical beings don’t always win. People choke and get inspired. If sports have a spirit, it’s in the recognition that our time is finite. We peak and we break down. Chances are, we’ll die. We have limited opportunities, so each failure is a little tragedy. What matters is the moment.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然比赛还早，但纪录缺失已经对Enhanced构成了灾难性的威胁。如果药物帮不了运动员举得更多，那么对我们其他人有什么意义呢？有趣的是，Santavy的失败反而证明了：在有药物的情况下，体育比赛同样可以保有戏剧性。Pound曾告诉我，兴奋剂会毁掉体育，因为它们实质上固定了结果：体育的关键在于结果本身的内在不确定性。我在想，WADA和Enhanced是否都高估了兴奋剂的帮助。体育之所以有趣，是因为更强壮的身体并不总能赢。人会失误，也会被激励。若体育有精神，那就是意识到我们的时间是有限的。我们会达到巅峰，也会崩溃。很可能，我们会死去。机会有限，所以每次失败都带着一点悲剧意味。真正重要的是这一刻。

paragraphs.en: Enhanced’s moment was turning out to be pretty boring. It was beginning to feel more like an exhibition than like a sporting event. Most of the weight lifters were in different weight classes, so they were competing against themselves. In the track events, there were three false starts, each of which typically means an automatic disqualification for the violator. But the runners simply lined up and went again. One runner decided to skip the preliminary heat and just run in the finals.
paragraphs.zh: Enhanced的这幕正在变得相当无聊。它开始更像一场展演，而不像一场真正的体育赛事。大多数举重选手分属不同级别，所以他们都在和自己较量。田赛道项目里有三次抢跑，每一次按常规都应让违规者直接取消资格。但跑者们只是重新排队再跑了。还有一名跑者选择跳过预赛，直接参加决赛。

paragraphs.en: The swimming events were more successful. Swimmers set sixteen personal bests. Megan Romano, the slot-machine player, won the women’s hundred-metre freestyle. She beat her previous best time by.28 seconds. Bizarrely, the Enhanced Games had allowed the swimmers to use “supersuits,” full-body swimsuits engineered with extra buoyancy that have been banned in mainstream competition. It was difficult to tell whether the personal bests were attributable more to the drugs or to the suits. And, still, no records.
paragraphs.zh: 游泳项目更成功一些。游泳运动员创下了16项个人最好成绩。玩“老虎机”的Megan Romano赢得了女子百米自由泳，她以0.28秒快于自己此前的最好成绩。奇怪的是，Enhanced Games允许游泳者使用“超人泳衣”，这种全身泳衣通过额外浮力来设计，在主流比赛中已被禁止。很难分辨这些个人最好成绩到底更多来自药物，还是来自泳衣。而且，依然没有任何纪录。

paragraphs.en: Chances came and went: the hundred-metre dash was a bust. Fred Kerley, the seventh-fastest man of all time, was the headliner for the men. He was serving a two-year track-and-field ban for missing three drug tests, but he said that he was competing clean. (“I don’t need it,” he explained.) He won, with a time of 9.97 seconds. Afterward, he said his enhanced competitors needed to “get on that shit a little more.” Yet his own time was slower than that of a high-school sophomore at this year’s Texas school championships. Hafþór Björnsson, the Icelandic man who played the Mountain on “Game of Thrones,” was trying to break his own world record for the dead lift, at eleven hundred and twenty-four pounds. But he failed, too. Angermayer had told me that he expected two to five world records. I ran into him midway through the Games. “I’m getting nervous,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 机会时有时无：百米短跑赛落了空。历史上第七快的弗雷德·克莱利（Fred Kerley）是男项头牌。他因缺席三次兴奋剂测试被禁赛两年，但他表示自己是清白上场的。他说：“我不需要这个。”（他这样解释道。）他以9.97秒赢得了比赛。赛后，他说其他增强型选手需要“再多上点那东西”。但他的成绩仍然慢于今年德州学校锦标赛上一位高中二年级生。冰岛人海夫托尔·比约恩松（Hafþór Björnsson，出演《权力的游戏》“The Mountain”）试图打破自己保持的硬拉世界纪录，目标是1124磅，但他也未能成功。安格迈尔告诉我他预计会有两到五项世界纪录。比赛进行到一半时我遇到了他。“我有点紧张，”他说。

paragraphs.en: I wondered if I could find some drugs. This was a crowd, mainly, of bio-hacking, sleep-obsessed healthmaxxers for whom the typical delights of Las Vegas held little appeal. Angermayer, who pointed out that alcohol, physically and societally, is much more unhealthy than anything the athletes were taking, told me he’d never had a drink in his life. Las Vegas’s economy has had to contend with a public whose interests are trending away from depravity and toward wellness. I was curious if the city’s market for substances had adapted.
paragraphs.zh: 我在想，我能不能找到一些药。这里几乎全是一群生物黑客、迷恋睡眠的健康极客，对拉斯维加斯常见的娱乐并不怎么感兴趣。安格迈尔指出，酒精在生理和社会层面都比运动员正在使用的任何东西更不健康，并告诉我他一辈子没喝过酒。拉斯维加斯的经济不得不面对一个兴趣越来越远离放纵、越来越偏向健康的公众。我很想看看这座城市的“兴奋剂市场”是否已经随之调整了。

paragraphs.en: I began following up my inquiries about enhancements with one about how to score some. Under the bleachers near the +LIQUIDS kiosk, I found myself in conversation with a group of content creators. A thirtysomething guy named Jake told me that he was an investor in Enhanced, which had just gone public, via a SPAC, or special-purpose acquisition company. “I own eight thousand shares,” he told me. “We have a mimetic economy. I’m, like, We’ve got some mimetic material here. Now, given the fact that we’ve broken no world records, I may have to sell immediately.” Jake was enhanced: “I actually know a guy who’s a big juice head. Former arm-wrestling world-champion contender. The guy told me when I went over his house, ‘I killed so many people’—he was in the Army. He said, ‘My way of doing good in the world is making people feel better.’ ” This involved peptides and anabolics. Jake asked if I wanted an intro.
paragraphs.zh: 我接着追问如何弄到这些增强物。就在看台下方的＋LIQUIDS服务台附近，我与一群内容创作者聊了起来。一位三十多岁的男士杰克告诉我，他是Enhanced的投资者，该公司刚刚通过SPAC（特殊目的收购公司）上市。“我持有八千股，”他说。“我们有一个拟态型的经济体系。也就是说，我们这里确实有一些拟态材料。现在，考虑到我们没有打破任何世界纪录，我可能得立即卖掉。”杰克本身也是增强型用户：“我其实认识一个‘重度补剂党’，以前是职业臂力角逐者、世界冠军争夺者。那家伙在我去他家时说：‘我杀过很多人’——他当过军人。然后他说：‘我觉得在这个世界上做好事的方式，就是让人们感觉更好。’”这牵涉到的是肽类和合成代谢激素。杰克问我是不是想要一次介绍。

paragraphs.en: I’ve never had a problem with athletes using pharmaceuticals. And yet I found myself rooting for the unenhanced athletes. I was happy when Armstrong won. I’m also very much unenhanced; I don’t even drink coffee. Among the syringe fillers, this gave me a feeling of superiority that I was a little ashamed of. D’Souza predicted that in the future there would be a small group of the unenhanced. “That will be a status symbol in itself,” he explained. I asked Jake what the enhancements felt like.
paragraphs.zh: 我从未认为运动员使用药物有问题。可我却不自觉地为未增强的运动员加油。当阿姆斯特朗赢了时，我很高兴。我本人也完全没有增强；我甚至不喝咖啡。在这些打针的人群里，这种感觉让我有一种略带优越感，甚至有点羞愧。德苏扎预测未来会出现一小撮未增强者。“那本身就是一种身份象征，”他解释道。我问杰克这些增强感受如何。

paragraphs.en: “Just imagine yourself but with ten to twenty per cent more energy,” he said. “Are you a calm, chill guy? Do you want to be less chill?”
paragraphs.zh: “你可以想象自己，能量增加百分之十到二十。”他说，“你是个冷静、从容的人吗？还是你想变得不那么‘冷静’？”

paragraphs.en: “Do I need to be less chill?” I asked.
paragraphs.zh: “我需要变得不那么冷静吗？”我问。

paragraphs.en: “I was very chill, but if you’ve never experienced being the guy who’s, like, ‘I will fight you!’ you should try it once in your life,” he said. “It’s pretty fun.”
paragraphs.zh: “我以前可是非常冷静的，但如果你从没体验过‘我要跟你打架了！’那种状态，那你一生都得至少试一次。”他说，“其实很有意思。”

paragraphs.en: I declined. The final event was about to begin. It was the fifty-metre freestyle. Last year, Kristian Gkolomeev, a decently credentialled Greek swimmer, had swum a record time at a private Enhanced event, while wearing a supersuit, a feat for which Enhanced paid him a million dollars. This March, Cameron McEvoy, an unenhanced, un-supersuited Australian, beat his time, at 20.88 seconds. If Gkolomeev could surpass that, he’d get himself another million. Fifty metres is just one length down the pool—a frenzy of thrashing arms and legs. It was going to be close. I felt genuine suspense. When Gkolomeev touched the wall, the clock said 20.81—a new record, of sorts. Martin, the C.E.O., had watched from the pool deck, and he hugged Gkolomeev, soaking his double-breasted olive suit.
paragraphs.zh: 我拒绝了。最后一项比赛即将开始。那是五十米自由泳。去年，拥有不俗履历的希腊泳手克里斯蒂安·格科洛梅耶夫（Kristian Gkolomeev）在一项非公开的增强组比赛中，以穿戴超级泳衣的成绩创造了纪录，这一成就让他拿到了100万美元。到了今年三月，澳大利亚选手卡梅伦·麦克伊（Cameron McEvoy）以20秒88，在没有使用增强、没有穿超级泳衣的情况下打破了他的成绩。如果格科洛梅耶夫能够超过这个成绩，他还会再拿到一百万美元。五十米只是一池子长度，干脆就是一场手足乱舞般的狂冲。比赛会很接近。我感到一种真实的悬念。当格科洛梅耶夫触碰到池壁时，计时器显示20秒81——某种意义上的新纪录。总裁马丁（Martin）站在池边看着，随后拥抱了格科洛梅耶夫，结果把那件双排扣橄榄绿色西装浸湿了。

paragraphs.en: There was a brief closing ceremony, at which Enhanced had arranged for the Killers to play a set. The front man, Brandon Flowers, called out, “Congratulations, whoever deserves it!” I looked for Jake, hoping to wish him well. Maybe the swimming record meant he’d avoid some stock losses. He was still under the bleachers when I found him. He hadn’t been watching. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 有一个简短的闭幕式，Enhanced 乐队安排了 《杀手》 来演奏一场。主唱 Brandon Flowers 说了句：“祝贺所有值得这份荣誉的人！”我在找 Jake，希望能向他致意。也许这次游泳纪录意味着他会少亏一些股票。他还躲在看台下，我找到他时，他并没有在看。♦
